channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money criminal case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but indicated he wouldn't be jailed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Excerpts from the judge's ruling to go ahead with Trump sentencing
item.description: The judge overseeing Donald Trump’s hush money case in New York dealt with a host of weighty, unprecedented questions before deciding Friday that the president-elect should be sentenced later this month for the crime of falsifying business records.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden blocks $14 billion acquisition of US Steel by Japan's Nippon Steel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has blocked the nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel — affirming his earlier vow on the presidential campaign trail to prevent the acquisition of Steeltown USA’s most storied steel company.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, JOSH BOAK and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Surgeon General calls for new label on drinks to warn Americans of alcohol's cancer risk
item.description: Alcohol is a leading cause of cancer, a risk that should be clearly labeled on drinks Americans consume, U.S. Surgeon General Vivek Murthy proposed on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Chip Roy bid for Rules chair emerges as key issue ahead of Speaker vote
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy’s (R-Texas) bid to be chair of the House Rules Committee is emerging as a key issue for those withholding support for Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) ahead of Friday afternoon’s Speaker election. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.), who has withheld support for Johnson, told former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: New Year's attacks stoke fears of political violence
item.description: A pair of fatal attacks on New Year's Day have reignited concerns over political violence in the U.S. after an election already marked by multiple assassination attempts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Conservatives seek commitments as Johnson scrambles for Speaker votes
item.description: Roughly a dozen hard-line House conservatives are demanding a host of amorphous commitments from Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) to win their support Friday, throwing his leadership tenure into uncertainty less than 24 hours before the vote.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Biden awards the Medal of Honor and Medal of Valor to military heroes and first responders
item.description: President Joe Biden has awarded the Medal of Honor to military recipients who displayed heroism and self-sacrifice in combat and the Medal of Valor to first responders who put their own lives at risk to save others.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jill Biden gets the priciest gift from a foreign leader in 2023
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamo
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With a nod to her mentor Shirley Chisholm, Rep. Barbara Lee exits Congress as a renegade herself
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Barbara Lee has always stood apart, a matter-of-fact renegade with a long list of firsts. In high school, she was the first Black student to integrate her Southern California cheerleading squad.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New laws in NJ impact minimum wage, data privacy, and reading content
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- With every new year come new laws -- each impacting someone somewhere in the Garden State. New Jersey's minimum wage is increasing to $15.49 an hour for most workers.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: New law takes effect to protect first responders
item.description: Several new laws go into effect for the 2025 New Year, and one of them is designed to protect first responders from threats.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 7 rising Democratic leaders to watch
item.description: Democrats are scrambling for a new leader to head the party and organize resistance to President-elect Trump following Vice President Harris’s loss in November's election.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls it the 'center of the universe.' Mar-a-Lago is a magnet for those seeking influence
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The cars begin lining up early in the morning to be screened by Secret Service agents under white tents near the fence that surrounds President-elect Donald Trump’s vast south Florida estate.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC
